observations.recommendation:
We strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to (a) coordinate with the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the preparation of the duly certified list showing the names of all taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year and cause submission to the Municipal Accountant as a basis of recording in the books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables and the equivalent Deferred Income accounts.
b. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of RPT and SET Receivables with the total delinquency taxes and effect the necessary adjustments, if any;
c. Municipal Accountant to prepare the correcting entries for the advance payment of RPT and adopt the new guidelines in recording the total amount collectible at the beginning of the year; and
d. Municipal Treasurer to monitor property tax payment of the current year and exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all delinquent real property taxes and encourage the tax payers to pay their dues on time.
observations.managementAction: An increase in the collection of real property tax due for the current year was noted in CY 2021.

The Municipal Engineer provided the Municipal Accountant and GSO with description and cost segregation of road components. Inventory Committee established. No property ledger cards were prepared and maintained. No report on Local Road Network was submitted.
observations.reason: No property ledger cards were prepared and maintained. Report on Local Road Network not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. consider the adoption of the procedures and guidelines on the one-time cleansing
of PPE prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006.

b. require the Municipal Accountant to prepare Property Ledger Cards for all PPEs.

c. require the Office of the Designated GSO to prepare Property Cards and Property
Acknowledgement Receipt for all PPEs.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the preparation of the
duly certified list showing the names of all taxpayers and the tax due from each of them
at the start of the year and cause submission to the Municipal Accountant as a basis of
recording in the books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables and the equivalent
Deferred Income accounts.

Further, the Municipal Treasurer ensure that the reported tax delinquency is the
complete and correct amount supported with the list of all the taxpayers and their
corresponding balances and submit the Municipal Accountant to be the basis of the
correcting entries for the RPT and SET Receivable balances as of the beginning of CY
2022.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to restore
to the Cash in Bank the amount of the unreleased checks and recognize proper liability
accounts at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct that the Municipal Treasurer effect
the cancellation of the stale checks and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the
original disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents for the preparation of
Journal Entry Voucher in the municipality’s books of accounts to fairly present its
financial statements as of December 31, 2021. Likewise, cause the reversion of
P741,461.41 to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, if warranted, upon
approval by the legislative body.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Budget Officer to revert the unexpended balances of the Maintenance and
Other Operating Expenses at the end of the fiscal year and made it available for
expenditure by subsequent enactment; and

b. Municipal Engineer, MPDC and MDRRMO to implement the planned
projects/programs/activities on time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
observe the proper journal entries for recording of receipts and disbursements under
the Trust Fund. Refrain from making shortcut entries for proper monitoring of the
balances of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor and members of the Municipal Development
Council to:

a. assess the feasibility of the implementation and responsiveness to the current
needs of the constituents of those unimplemented projects; and

b. ensure optimal and timely utilization of 20% Development Fund in the succeeding
periods by including development projects that are well-planned and procurement-
and-implementation-ready pursuant to DBM, DOF and DILG Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2020-01.

We strongly reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned municipal
officials to effect the actual cash transfers of all unexpended balance of the LDRRMF
for QRF and MF-MOOE for CYs 2018-2021 and every year thereafter to the Trust Fund
books pursuant to the above regulation.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant, Budget
Officer and MDRRMO to verify and ascertain the actual status of the unexpended
LDRRMF amounting to P4,762,902.60 and adjust records accordingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make
the necessary journal entry to present the correct year-end balances of the affected
accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality. Henceforth, record under the
Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account the DRRM funds received from National Government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to make the
necessary adjustments on their records regarding the above noted additional LDRRMF
to reflect the appropriate amount of funds to be used in disaster mitigation,
preparedness, prevention and response activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strongly require the Office of the Municipal
Accountant, Budget Officer and MDRRMO to reconcile records as regards the above
noted deficiencies. Hereafter, ensure that succeeding reports were already tally with
other office’s record to present fairly the related accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide a suitable size stockroom with
installed racks that will be enough for ten (10) years mandatory document custody and
safeguarding of the Municipality’s accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. consider the adoption of the procedures and guidelines on the one-time cleansing of PPE prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006.
b. require the Municipal Accountant to prepare Property Ledger Cards for all PPEs.
c. require the Office of the Designated GSO to prepare Property Cards and Property Acknowledgement Receipt for all PPEs.
observations.managementAction:
Inventory Committee was created Thru Memorandum Order No. 2021-087 dated June 22, 2021.
The Designated GSO utilized different format of Inventory Reports.
Not Only the Office Equipment and Motor Vehicles were accounted.
JEV No. 01-01-002 - P1,904,642.00; JEV No. 01-01-003 - P1,941,064.00; JEV No. 01-01-004 - P1,929,294.00; JEV No. 01-01-005 - P1,941,064.00; JEV No. 01-01-006 - P1,929,294.00; JEV No. 01-01-007 - P1,904,642.00 dated January 31, 2021 totaling P11,550,000.00
An appropriation amounting to P867,756.00 was provided for the filling up of the General Government Department Head (GSO).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to exert more effort to identify the sources of the accounts and reconcile the accounts; and
b. afterwhich, if the issue remain unsettled, resort to prescribed procedures on the write-off of receivables and/or payables upon presentation of the required documentation.
observations.managementAction: A discrepancy of P694,370.79 between the two Accounts was still noted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to fully coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and have a direct sit-down review and analysis of the accounts to determine the causes of the long overdue deficiencies. If claims are fully documented and establishes regularity of the transactions, then settlements/collections should be made by the Municipality. In case exhaustion of all possible remedies and diligent efforts, the issue remains unsettled, resort to prescribed procedures on write-off of receivables and/or reversion of accounts payables upon presentation of the required documentation.
observations.managementAction: Accounts, they analyzed those accounts, they determined that the causes of the long overdue deficiencies. In case of settlements, then settlements/collections should be made by the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Provincial Accountant's staff and determined that the causes of the long overdue deficiencies were from old accounts.
observations.reason: The accounts cannot be just reverted to unappropriated surplus because there is still possibility of claims related to those accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to deduct the correct amount of premiums and remit the same immediately to avoid incurrence of penalties and remittance of correct amount of premiums and loan amortization to GSIS;
b. Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to ensure employee's net pay which is not less than P5,000.00 after deducting the loan amortizations prior to approving loans; and
c. Municipal Accountant and AAO to work hand in hand and coordinate with the GSIS for the reconciliation of records to determine the correctness of the unpaid premiums of PS and GS with a total amount of P172,118.64.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and AAO complied to the required reconciliation of the accounts and the reconciliation of records has been done.

observations.recommendation:
With the foregoing lapses and deviations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to present for audit the lacking documents to substantiate aforementioned BGCM transactions and ensure that all documentary requirements for emergency purchases are complied with and properly filled out before certifying as to completeness of the supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers; and
b. Municipal Accountant to use the appropriate inventory accounting in recording transactions in compliance with Section 188, Volume I of the GAM for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government of Ibaan responded immediately. The Municipal Accountant forwarded the lacking documents to the Audit Team last June 15, 2021. The Municipal Accountant used the appropriate inventory accounting in compliance with Section 188, Volume I of the GAM for LGUs.

observations.recommendation: Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to provide justification on the delay of actual submission of liquidation documents and on the result of the validation of the beneficiaries showing some ineligibles.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted documents. The MSWDO furnished the Audit Team of its transmittal letter to DSWD Region IV-A. The MSWDO provided justification on the delay of actual submission of liquidation documents and on the result of the validation of the beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation:
With the foregoing lapses on the planning, appropriation and utilization, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Development Council to plan and identify the projects to be included in the Annual Investment Plan; 
b. Municipal Budget Officer to avoid/refrain from timely preparation of budgets for projects in lump sum. For projects with lumpsum appropriation, secure the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan before obligation and implementation; and
c. Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to monitor closely the implementation of the projects and ensure maximum utilization of the funds available through 100% completion and accomplishments of program, projects and activities due the public.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Development Council (MDC) planned and identified projects for CY 2021. The Municipal Budget timely prepared the budget in lumpsum. The Municipal Engineer submitted justification letter dated June 15, 2021 on the reasons for the delay on the implementation of projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
LDRRM Officer to: a. complete the preparation of the Municipality’s long-term LDRRMP which should be fully integrated with the Local Development Plan and Comprehensive Land-Use Plan for review and evaluation of the Office of the Civil Defense; b. ensure that the projects and activities charged to the LDRRMF remained unexpended; c. make certain the maximum utilization of current Mitigation Fund; d. make the necessary reversion of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF Special Trust Fund amounting to P4,762,902.60 which remained unutilized for more than five years from Trust Fund to General Fund after subsequent enactment of local Sanggunian; e. effect the recognition and transfer of cash from the General Fund to the Trust Fund Book of the unutilized QRF for CYs 2018-2019 and MF-MOOE for CY 2019, in the total amount of P5,486,174.63 to reconcile different records; f. properly account the remaining balance of QRF, MF-MOOE and MF-CO for CY 2020 and unexpended QRF and immediately transfer them to appropriate account/appropriations; g. reconcile records regarding the outstanding balance of unexpended QRF and MF-MOOE remaining under the Trust Liabilities – DRRM account; h. submit and duly account to the Audit Team cash donations received during the Taal Volcano eruption and the spread of COVID-19 virus for audit to ensure compliance with Item IV.B of COA Circular No. 2014-002.
Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, LDRRM Officer and Budget Officer to: a. make the necessary adjustments on their respective records; b. effect the transfer of funds; c. reconcile records; d. explain the deficit amounting to P6,494.68 on unexpended CY 2015-2019 QRF after the replan of P10,540,000.00; e. reconcile records regarding the outstanding balance of unexpended QRF and MF-MOOE remaining under the Trust Liabilities – DRRM account; and f. submit documents to substantiate the transactions.
observations.managementAction:
a. LDRRM Plan 2020-2030 was approved by Sangguniang Bayan and Office of Civil Defense on December 29, 2021 and April 11, 2022, respectively.
b. Mitigation Fund was almost fully utilized while disbursements were made as well out of the Continuing Appropriations.
c. The Municipal Accountant transferred interest earned to the General Fund. Monthly report was prepared in CY 2021.
d. The Municipal Accountant to use appropriate inventory accounts in recording transactions in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.
e. Reconciliation and transfers of unexpended QRF and MF-MOOE for CYs 2018-2021 were to be effected; documents were submitted to substantiate transactions.
observations.reason: Records remain not reconciled; inconsistencies observed among offices. Some documents were still lacking and follow-up audit verification required.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. GSO-Designate to: i. number each type of supplies and maintain Stock Cards per stock number; ii. forward copy of the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the DV and recording of deliveries in the appropriate ledger cards; iii. consolidate weekly the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for supplies and materials issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant together with the original copies of RIS; and iv. submit the Report on Physical Count of Inventories to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.
b. Municipal Accountant to: i. use the appropriate inventory account in recording the purchases and corresponding expense account upon receipt of SSMI submitted by the GSO; and ii. use the moving average method in costing the inventories.
Also recommended filling up of permanent GSO position.
observations.managementAction: GSO submitted Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) weekly to the Municipal Accountant. Acceptance and Inspection Reports were submitted to Municipal Accountant. Supplies were recorded as expense because supplies were immediately consumed. Municipal Accountant to use appropriate inventory account recorded as expense. Moving average method to be used. No permanent GSO filled yet.
observations.reason: Supplies were immediately expensed; stock cards and complete SSMI consolidation not fully implemented. Permanent GSO position not yet filled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned municipal officials to comply strictly with the policy, rules and regulations on the granting of incentives and awards and ensure that the rules, specific policies and guidelines in their performance planning, monitoring and evaluation and mechanism for recognizing the awardees are in place before granting awards to the officials and employees.
Also ensure monetary awards given only when accomplishments resulted in monetary savings and do not exceed 20% of the savings generated.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted documents to justify their grant of personnel incentive. The Municipality justified that most of the office supplies and purchases were not available from DBM-PS and that purchases were made accordingly.
observations.reason: No specific guidelines or manual yet designed by the Municipality to fully substantiate grants under PRAISE; lacking clear basis for amount given.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer to comply with the regulations that ordinary and regular office supplies shall be procured from the Procurement Service of the DBM and shopping from private suppliers should be resorted to only for items not available in the PS-DBM and supported with Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks from the PS-DBM.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant justified that most of the office supplies were not available from PS-DBM and shopping was resorted to private suppliers. Certificates of Non-Availability were provided where applicable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare individual financial statements with subsidiary ledgers for each economic enterprise being operated by the Municipality and for its 20% Development Fund which should form schedules of the General Fund in accordance with the above cited regulations; and require the Municipal Budget Officer in coordination with the Head of the economic enterprise to continuously appropriate different funds for Waterworks and Cemetery but separate the appropriation for Market and Slaughterhouse in the ensuing year to facilitate the reconciliation with the records of the Accounting office.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant stated that individual subsidiary ledgers were not prepared. Municipal Budget Officer was continuously appropriating funds for Waterworks and Cemetery. Appropriation for Market and Slaughterhouse were not separated.
observations.reason: Individual financial statements and subsidiary ledgers for economic enterprises were not prepared; appropriations not separated as recommended.

observations.managementAction: A MENRO-Designate was overseeing the MSWM activities. Mainstreaming of GAD and other programs were in progress. MENRO designate and HRMO actions being undertaken. Proposal for MENRO position included in devolution and transition plan. DENR-EMB Region 4A evaluation ongoing.
observations.reason: MENRO position not yet fully established due to budgetary constraints; full compliance to RA 9003 pending final evaluations and approvals.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as Chairperson of the Executive Committee together with the Gender Focal Point System and the Technical Working Group:
a. conduct gender analysis during the preparation of GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report using Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) Tool; b. secure first the Certificate of Endorsement of the Annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) from the DILG Provincial Office before incorporation to Municipality’s Annual Budget; c. establish and maintain GAD Database pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and d. ensure the mainstreaming of GAD related activities in all departments/offices of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: GAD Focal orientation on Gender Analysis and GAD Plan and Guidelines were conducted. GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2021 was reviewed and the Certificate of Endorsement from DILG was obtained September 2, 2022. MSWDO submitted a copy of Community Poverty Indicator Monitoring System as of December 31, 2021. Some mainstreaming of GAD noted.
observations.reason: Some gender issues in GPB not supported by database; full mainstreaming and database establishment still in progress. Implementation to continue in CY 2022.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Praise Committee:
a. comply strictly with the policy, rules and documents for PRAISE to ensure propriety and regularity of any actions taken out of the Program; b. ensure that the rules, specific policies and guidelines in their performance planning, monitoring and evaluation and mechanism for recognizing the awardees are in place before granting awards to the officials and employees; and c. consider individual appraisal or evaluation report assessing the performance of employees by means of the agency’s established system of incentives and awards. Ensure monetary awards given only when the accomplishments resulted in monetary savings and do not exceed 20% of the savings generated.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted documents to justify the grant of CY 2018 personnel incentive. They contended that the award was granted under PRAISE program and provided documentation, but no established manual yet.
observations.reason: No specific guidelines or established system/formal manual yet; documentation submitted did not fully justify basis for grant as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the accounts, determine the validity of payables and request related authority from the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of these long outstanding payables to unappropriated surplus.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed and analyzed accounts; they could not be just reverted to unappropriated surplus because there is still possibility of claims related to those accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to verify and evaluate the accounts involved in the above transactions and to prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the adjustments for the stale checks to present the correct and reliable balance of the Cash in Bank and Accounts Payable accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Verification and evaluation undertaken; required adjustments to be prepared where applicable.
observations.reason: Some stale checks remain; full cancellation and adjusting entries pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to pursue the titling of the 14 parcels of land in the name of the Municipality in order to establish absolute ownership over the said property; and require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts presented in the financial statements. Also provide appropriation for the cost of titling the land.
observations.managementAction: Three parcels of land already titled; three parcels waiting for release of title (2nd quarter of year 2022); others on process with CENRO. Appropriation for titling was provided in CY 2020. Municipal Assessor and Municipal Accountant coordinating; Municipal Accountant to disclose in Notes to FS once titles finalized.
observations.reason: Titling of the parcels is ongoing; some parcels remain untitled and documentation not yet fully available for disclosure.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to analyze and reconcile non-moving balances and other related accounts to determine their proper disposition. We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan pass a Resolution requesting proper write-off of receivables from KKK proponents and submit to the Office of the Auditor with proof of efforts to recover the amounts.
observations.managementAction: Review and reconciliation undertaken; amounts reduced minimally. Recommended submission of resolution for write-off where applicable.
observations.reason: Old records and documentation incomplete; reconciliation and write-off process ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to verify and evaluate the accounts involved and prepare Journal Entry Voucher to effect adjustments for the stale checks to present the correct balances.
observations.managementAction: No adjustment was made for the stale checks; verification of documents is ongoing.
observations.reason: Verification and documentary review not yet performed

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. create/reconstitute an Inventory Committee for the conduct of complete and
detailed physical count of all the Municipality’s properties taking into account the
provisions mandated under COA Circular 2020-006, particularly on the one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts;
b. instruct the Inventory Committee to properly utilize the required inventory reports
stated on the above-mentioned regulations;
c. require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate to:
i. work coordinately with the assistance of the Municipal Engineer on the
inventory of Infrastructure Assets such as Land Improvements, Local Road
Network, Flood Control System, Water Supply System and Other Public
Infrastructures;
ii. exert efforts on all causes of discrepancies towards the reconciliation of
their respective records to ascertain the accuracy of the PPE balances in
the financial statements;
d. enjoin the Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary accounting entries to
book up the acquired Motor Vehicles; and
e. instruct the Municipal Budget Officer for the enactment of the Sangguniang Bayan
to appropriate funds in ensuing year for the filling up of the General Services
Officer to have a permanent official who will assume the duties and responsibilities
in the office and to address the perennial issues relative to the supply and property
management of the Municipality.

We strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the
preparation of the duly certified list showing the names of all taxpayers and the tax
due from each of them at the start of the year and cause submission to the Municipal
Accountant as a basis of recording in the books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables
and the equivalent Deferred Income accounts;
b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances of RPT
and SET Receivables with the total delinquency taxes and effect the necessary
adjustments, if any;
c. Municipal Accountant to prepare the correcting entries for the advance payment of RPT
and adopt the new guidelines in recording the total amount collectible at the beginning
of the year; and
d. Municipal Treasurer to monitor payment of real property tax due for the current year
and exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all delinquent real property taxes
and encourage the tax payers to pay their dues on time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant:
a. to exert more effort to identify the sources of the unreconciled difference between the
Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds through reconciliation of the accounts; and
b. afterwhich, if the issue remain unsettled, resort to prescribed procedures on write-
off of receivables and/or payables upon presentation of the required
documentation.

We strongly reiterated that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant
to exert time and effort to fully coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and have a
direct sit-down review and analysis of the accounts to determine the causes of the
long overdue deficiencies. If claims are fully documented and establishes regularity of
transactions, then settlements/collections should be made by the Municipality. In case
exhaustion of all possible remedies and diligent efforts, the issue remains unsettled,
resort to prescribed procedures on write-off of receivables and/or reversion of
accounts payables upon presentation of the required documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to deduct the correct amount of premiums and loan
amortizations due to avoid incurrence of penalties and remit the same immediately
to GSIS;
b. Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to ensure compliance with the required
employee’s net pay after deducting the loan amortizations prior to approving
loans; and
c. Municipal Accountant and AAO to work hand in hand and coordinate with the GSIS
for the reconciliation of records to determine the correctness of the unpaid
premiums of PS and GS with a total amount of ₱172,118.64.

We praise the Management of the Municipal Government of Ibaan for the competent
and rapid implementation of the identified programs, activities and projects towards
the achievement of BGCM’s objective.

However, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the BAC, thru its Secretariat to immediately present for audit
the lacking documents to substantiate aforementioned BGCM transactions and ensure
that all documentary requirements for emergency purchase are complied with and
properly filled out before certifying as to completeness of the supporting documents of
the disbursement vouchers; and
b. Municipal Accountant to use the appropriate accounting in recording transactions in
compliance with Section 188, Volume I of the GAM for LGUs.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to provide
justification on the delay of actual submission of liquidation documents and on the
result of the validation of the beneficiaries showing some ineligibles.

With the foregoing lapses on the planning, appropriation and utilization, we
recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Development Council to plan and identify specifically the projects to be
included in the Annual Investment Plan;
b. Municipal Budget Officer to avoid/refrain preparation of budgets for projects in
lumpsum appropriation. For projects with lumpsum appropriation, secure the
approval of the Sangguniang Bayan before obligation and implementation; and
c. Municipal Engineer and Municipal Development Coordinator to monitor closely the
implementation of projects and ensure maximum utilization of the funds available
through 100% completion and accomplishments of program, projects and
activities due the public.

We would like to laud the efforts undertaken by the MDRRMO and the whole
Municipality in responding to the different calamities and disasters that struck not
only the Municipality but also its nearby areas. However, for the above noted
deficiencies, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• LDRRM Officer to:
a. complete the preparation of the Municipality’s long-term LDRRMP which should
be fully integrated with the Local Development Plan and Comprehensive Land-
Use Plan for review and evaluation of the Office of the Civil Defense;
b. ensure that the list of projects and activities which are to be charged to the
unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF- under the Trust Fund books, is included
in the Annual LDRRMF Investment Plan to be prepared yearly; and
c. make certain the maximum utilization of current Mitigation Funds and coordinate
with the concerned Municipal department for the implementation of planned
programs/projects of the LDRRM Office under the Continuing Appropriations.
• Municipal Accountant to:
a. effect the transfer of cash equivalent to the interest earned totaling ₱47,891.08
from the Trust Fund to the General Fund so that the amount could be made
available for appropriation;
b. prepare and certified correct the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
LDRRMF to be submitted by the LDRRM Officer to the Audit Team on or before
the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local
Development Council;
c. use the appropriate inventory accounts in recording transactions in compliance
with COA Circular No. 2015-009 prescribing the Revised Chart of Accounts for
Local Government Units and Volume III of the new Government Accounting
Manual (GAM) for LGUs; and
d. comply with the recording of revenues and expenditures in the Trust Fund
books as provided under the new GAM for LGUs.
• Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, LDRRM Officer and Budget Officer to:
a. make the necessary reversion of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF
amounting to ₱4,762,902.60 which remained unutilized for more than five years
from Trust Fund to General Fund after subsequent enactment of local
Sanggunian;
b. effect the recognition and transfer of cash from the General Fund to the Trust
Fund Book of the unutilized QRF for CYs 2018-2019 and MF-MOOE for CY 2019,
in the total amount of ₱5,486,174.63 to reconcile different records;
c. properly account the remaining balance of QRF, MF-MOOE and MF-CO for CY
2020 and immediately transfer them to appropriate account/appropriations;
d. reconcile records regarding the outstanding of balance of the Continuing
Appropriations;
e. explain the deficit arrived at amounting to ₱6,494.68 on unexpended CY 2015-
2019 QRF after the replan of ₱10,540,000.00;
f. reconcile records regarding the outstanding balance of unexpended QRF and
MF-MOOE remaining under the Trust Liabilities – DRRM account; and
g. submit and duly account to the Audit Team cash donations received during the
Taal Volcano eruption and the spread of COVID-19 virus for audit to ensure
compliance with Item IV.B of COA Circular No. 2014-002.
• Municipal Accountant and BAC, thru its Secretariat to:
a. immediately present for audit the lacking documents to substantiate
aforementioned transactions and/or provide written explanation/justification for
the incomplete documentation especially the lacking Notice of Award and
Notice to Proceed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. General Services Officer-Designate to: Conduct complete inventory taking and submit Report on Physical Count of PPE and effect disposition for unserviceable properties as prescribed; b. Municipal Accountant to take up in the books of account the appropriate amount for depreciation of the aforementioned PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: The GSO-Designate is also the LDRRMO who at the time of Taal Volcano eruption and COVID-19 pandemic is one of the frontliners. Reiterated in Part II of this Report, page no. 42.
observations.reason: LDRRM Officer - Designate is the same person and the same person is one of the frontliners; lack of personnel

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectibles at the beginning of the year as basis for the setting up of receivables to ensure reliability of the balances of RPT and SET Receivable accounts; and b. devise strategies to improve collection efficiency.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already reconciled and effected the adjustment to the Real Property Tax Receivable; the Municipal Assessor is coordinating for the computerized RPT system; the Municipal Treasurer is in process of reconciling with the Municipal Assessor to ensure accuracy of the certified list.
observations.reason: Awaiting the full implementation of the computerized real property tax assessment system; Lack of personnel; process ongoing

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accounting Office to prepare and maintain the prescribed Registry of Local Road Networks and disclose the information for the said property in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the above cited regulations; b. General Services Office to prepare and maintain road networks property cards and to submit Report on Local Road Network; c. Municipal Engineer to provide the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; d. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory of all Municipality’s road network systems and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as of the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: Process has started and takes time to comply. Inventory Committee has been created, but completion of the inventory taking was not completed. The GSO-Designate is at the same time the LDRRMO. Records are being reconciled to new acquisitions. The Municipal Accountant and GSO are doing the reconciliation of records and are in the process of preparing the required reports.
observations.reason: Inventory has been initiated but not yet completed; Lack of personnel; delays due to concurrent responsibilities

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. GSO-Designate to: comply with the regulations that ordinary and regular office supplies and equipment involving amounts below the threshold should first be procured from PS-DBM; b. General Services Office to maintain Stock Cards per stock number and maintain Requisition and Issue Slips for supplies issued and submit SSMI to the Municipal Accountant; c. Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards per stock number and record purchases in the appropriate ledger cards upon receipt and use actual acquisition moving average cost method for inventories.
observations.managementAction: Supplies Ledger Cards are being maintained now by the Accounting Office. A job-order employee is assigned to do the job. The Municipal Accountant is in the process of reconciling and recording purchases. The GSO is to maintain SSMI and RIS; the Municipal Accountant is to record entries.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel in the GSO to perform inventory tasks; process undertaken but not fully completed

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accountant to: a. reconcile its records on receivables/payables in the Municipality periodically; and b. continue coordination with the Provincial Accountant for reconciliation and seek reversion or write-off if necessary and request Sangguniang Bayan approval for reversion of undocumented payables after conferring with the Provincial Accountant.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed and analyzed those accounts, reconciliation has started. The Management has acted on the reconciliation of accounts, and there is continuous reconciliation being made. Approval of the Sangguniang Bayan is yet to be sought upon full reconciliation of records.
observations.reason: Full reconciliation still in progress; awaiting supporting documents and approvals; lack of full documentation for some old items

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. comply with the policy rules and specific regulations on performance planning and monitoring prior to granting awards; b. ensure rules, policies and guidelines are in place before granting awards; c. consider individual appraisal/evaluation report assessing the performance of employees when granting awards.
observations.managementAction: The management had submitted documents to justify their grant of personnel incentives. They indicated that they have existing program and submitted documents that they claim justify the grant. The Municipal Mayor and Praise Committee: compliance being pursued.
observations.reason: No specific guidelines or manual designed by the Municipality; justification documents submitted but absence of clear support that accomplishments resulted in monetary savings over 20%

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. comply with the regulations that ordinary and regular office supplies and equipment involving amounts below the threshold should first be procured from PS-DBM; and b. prudently plan procurement each quarter and incorporate them in the APP to adopt competitive bidding.
observations.managementAction: Procurement for each quarter was planned and translated into Annual Procurement Plan. The Municipal Procurement plan was prepared though some purchases were done due to exigency during the pandemic. Compliance with PS-DBM procurement will be observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. require the Accounting Office to prepare individual financial statements with subsidiary ledgers for each economic enterprise and for its 20% Development Fund users which should form part of the schedules of the General Fund; and b. instruct the Municipal Budget Officer in coordination to continuously appropriate different funds for Waterworks and Cemetery and to separate appropriation for Market and Slaughterhouse in the ensuing year to facilitate reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is yet to comply with the recommendation fully. The Municipal Budget Officer will coordinate and segregate appropriations in the ensuing budget. Some actions were initiated to revise schedules.
observations.reason: Lack of accounting personnel; pending full compliance and preparation of separate financial schedules

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. ensure the 20% Local Development Fund appropriated out of IRA be optimally utilized for identified DF projects; and b. implement all projects efficiently and effectively, so that the benefits are enjoyed by the public on time.
observations.managementAction: Projects are being implemented; however, at year-end projects were still ongoing. A number of continuing appropriation projects were completed in 2020 though many under current appropriation were still unimplemented due to pandemic and other technical issues.
observations.reason: Delay due to COVID-19 pandemic and other technical issues such as right-of-way and site acquisition; some projects completed under continuing appropriations but not under current appropriations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. prioritize and undertake the necessary action to complete the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan as prescribed by R.A. 9003; b. reorganize its Municipal Solid Waste Management Board with strict compliance on membership and prepare and submit a plan for safe and sanitary management of solid waste; and c. create the position of Municipal Environmental Officer.
observations.managementAction: Reorganization was executed per Executive Order No. 2020-13 dated July 28, 2020. The draft of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan was submitted to EMB-DENR on June 8, 2021 for review and approval. Creation of Municipal Environmental Officer pending due to PS limitations.
observations.reason: Delay in final approval of 10-year plan; position creation pending due to Personal Services limitations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: LDRRM Officer to complete the long-term LDRRMP and include previous years’ unexpended LDRRMF projects in the annual LDRRMFIP and maximize utilization; Municipal Accountant to effect transfer of interest earned ₱47,891.08 from Trust Fund to General Fund prepare monthly reports, use appropriate inventory accounts and record revenues/expenditures in Trust Fund books; Municipal Accountant, Treasurer, LDRRM Officer and Budget Officer to revert unexpended balance of ₱4,762,902.60 older than five years to General Fund, effect recognition/transfer of cash for unutilized QRF for CYs 2018-2019 and MF-MOOE for CY 2019 of ₱5,486,174.63, properly account remaining balances and reconcile records; Municipal Accountant and BAC Secretariat to present lacking documents for transactions and provide explanations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer presented list of cash donations received; the Municipal Accountant indicated interest earned ₱47,891.08 were transferred to General Fund; they will submit monthly reports; LDRRM Officer stated the long-term LDRRM Plan is 60% complete and forwarded plan to Audit Team; Municipal Budget Officer provided justifications on projects and committed to transfer balances and reconcile records.
observations.reason: Some actions taken (transfer of interest, submission of donations list, draft LDRRMP at 60%); reconciliation of Continuing Appropriations and Trust Liabilities still pending; deficits and discrepancies need reconciliation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. conduct complete physical count of PPE and submit RPCPPE; b. prepare ledgers for RPT/SET receivables; c. devise strategies to improve collection; and d. reconcile and write-off dormant balances and seek Sangguniang Bayan approval for write-off where appropriate.
observations.managementAction: The GSO to conduct inventory; Inventory Committee has been created; reconciliation of RPT/SET is ongoing; the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary write-off requests where documentation not found; some reclassification and reconciliation done.
observations.reason: Inventory and titling work ongoing; IT system for RPT being developed; dormant accounts require write-off authorization and supporting documentation

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. reconcile the difference between RPT Receivable and Delinquency and maintain subsidiary ledgers; b. Municipal Treasurer to submit certified list of taxpayers to the Accounting Office at start of year; and c. formulate strategies to intensify collection efforts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reconciled and adjusted the receivables; the Municipal Assessor and Treasurer are working on computerized system and reconciliation; instructions given to responsible personnel.
observations.reason: Awaiting computerized RPT system; reconciliation ongoing

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to formulate additional strategies to enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes and intensify collection campaigns to generate more income.
observations.managementAction: Instructions were given to the Municipal Treasurer to prepare additional strategies; coordination with Assessor and IT for system improvements ongoing.
observations.reason: Strategies being developed; IT system pending

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the payable accounts and request Sangguniang Bayan approval for reversion of long outstanding payables to unappropriated surplus after determining validity.
observations.managementAction: Accounts analyzed; some could not be supported; management to seek reversion via Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.reason: Some items under review; documentation lacking for certain long outstanding payables

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO to conduct physical count and submit RPCPPE and for Municipal Accountant to maintain ledgers and reconcile.
observations.managementAction: The GSO-Designate conducted initial inventory; Committee established; partial actions undertaken.
observations.reason: Inventory initiated but RPCPPE not fully submitted; workload and manpower constraints

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit certified list of taxpayers at beginning of year and Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers and adopt strategies to improve collection and computerize RPT.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reconciled receivables and is awaiting IT system; Municipal Treasurer and Assessor coordinating.
observations.reason: Awaiting computerized system; reconciliation ongoing

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Treasurer to design strategies to enforce collection of delinquent taxes and intensify collection campaigns.
observations.managementAction: Instructions given; processes being instituted to enhance collection and IT improvements planned.
observations.reason: Ongoing; pending full implementation of strategies and system

observations.recommendation: We recommended reconciliation and seeking Sangguniang Bayan approval for reversion where appropriate.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed and analyzed accounts; coordination with Provincial Accounting Office ongoing; some write-off requests to be prepared.
observations.reason: Reconciliation not fully completed; some items lack documentation

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Assessor to pursue titling of the 14 parcels and the Municipal Mayor to provide appropriation for titling costs; Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary JE to reflect correct balances.
observations.managementAction: Assigned personnel to do leg work; titling documents in process; mode of acquisition and valuation basis being reviewed; appropriation considered in CY 2020 budget.
observations.reason: Processing of required documents ongoing; appropriation reconsidered; delay in titling due to documentation and procedural requirements

observations.recommendation: We recommended analyze and reconcile non-moving balances and seek Sangguniang Bayan approval for write-off of receivables from KKK proponents and submit proof of recovery efforts.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed and reconciled accounts; preparation of write-off requests to Sangguniang Bayan to be made where documentation absent.
observations.reason: Old records not readily available; write-off requests pending and will be submitted after thorough review

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. General Services Officer-Designate (GSO) together with the Inventory Committee
conduct a complete physical count of all the PPEs of the Municipality, work on the
necessary disposal of unserviceable properties, and exert efforts toward
reconciliation with the Accounting Office and submit the same in the prescribed
format to the Audit Team; and
b. Municipal Accountant to take up in the books of accounts the appropriate amount
for deprecation of the aforementioned PPE accounts.
Considering that PPE accounts signifies 64% of the total asset of the Municipality, we
also recommended to the Municipal Mayor the filling up of a permanent official on the
Office of the General Services who will assume the duties and responsibilities of the
Office and who will address the perennial issues relative to the supplies and property
management of the Municipality.

We strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Assessor to provide the Municipal Treasurer with the updated and duly
verified assessment roll of the Municipality’s taxable properties;
b. Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the preparation of the duly certified list showing the
names of all taxpayers and the tax due from each of them at the start of the year
and cause submission to the Municipal Accountant as a basis of recording in the
books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables and the equivalent Deferred Income
accounts; and
c. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of RPT, SET Receivables and their
reciprocal account and total delinquency tax as prepared by the Municipal Treasurer
and effect the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger
cards for Local Road Networks and disclose the information for the said property
in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the above cited
regulations;
b. General Services Office to prepare and maintain road networks property cards
and to submit Report on Local Road Network;
c. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and GSO with the
complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
and
d. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s road
network systems and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical Count of the
Road Network System as of the end of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Municipal GSO-Designate to:
a. number each type of supplies and maintain Stock Cards per stock number;
b. forward copy of the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) to the Municipal
Accountant for the preparation of the DV and recording of deliveries in the
appropriate ledger cards;
c. consolidate weekly the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for which supplies and
materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued
(SSMI) and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant together with the
original copies of RIS; and
d. submit the Report on Physical Count of Inventories to the Office of the Auditor
not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.
• Municipal Accountant to:
a. maintain Supplies Ledger Cards per stock number and record the purchases in
the appropriate ledger cards upon receipt of the AIR and other supporting
documents;
b. use the appropriate inventory account in recording the purchases and
corresponding expense account upon receipt of SSMI submitted by the GSO;
and
c. use the moving average method in costing the inventories.
Similar with that of the PPE, we repeated our recommendation to the Municipal Mayor
regarding the filling up of the General Services Officer position to have a permanent
official who will assume the duties and responsibilities in the Office, and to address
in totality the recurrent issues relative to the supply and property management of the
Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accountant to:
a. reconcile its records on receivables/payables in the books of the Municipality for
Intra-agency accounts;
b. continue the coordination with the Provincial Accounting Office until such time
that the accounts be reconciled. If claims are fully documented and establishes
regularity of transactions, then settlements/collections should be made by the
Municipality. For those undocumented collectibles, where after all diligent efforts
that had been exhausted by the Municipal Accountant still disclosed zero basis,
then proper procedures leading to write-off of these accounts should be
considered; and
c. seek approval from the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of undocumented
payables to surplus accounts after conferring with the Provincial Accountant
showed the absence of any documents to prove validity of claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned municipal officials
and employees to refund to the amount of ₱10,000.00 each, despite disapproval of
Supplemental Budget by the Provincial Local Finance Committee as enacted by
Sangguniang Panlalawigan Resolution/Ordinance No. 155 Year 2020 and contrary with
the provisions of Administrative Order (AO) No. 19 dated December 2, 2019,
implemented by DBM Circular No. 2019 – 6, dated December 9, 2019, on the basis
that the Municipality has exceeded the 45% PS limitation.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer-
Designate to:
a. comply with the regulations that procurement of ordinary and regular office
supplies and equipment involving amounts below the threshold should first be
procured from DBM. Shopping from private suppliers should be resorted to, only
for items not available in the PS-DBM. Thus, in case shopping is resorted to, the
claim should be supported with a Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks from the
PS-DBM; and
b. prudently and judiciously plan procurement for each quarter as consolidated and
incorporate them in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) in order to adopt
competitive public bidding to provide reasonable assurance that the best quality at
the lowest price is availed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Municipal Accountant to prepare individual financial statements with
complete subsidiary ledgers for each economic enterprise being operated by the
Municipality and for its 20% Development Fund which should form part of the
schedules of the General Fund in accordance with the above cited regulations;
and
b. instruct the Municipal Budget Officer in coordination with the Head of the economic
enterprise to continuously appropriate different funds for Waterworks and Cemetery
but separate the appropriation for Market and Slaughterhouse in the ensuing year to
facilitate the reconciliation with the records of the Accounting office.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Engineer and
Municipal Development Coordinator to:
a. ensure the 20% Local Development Fund appropriated out of Internal Revenue
Allotment be optimally utilized and identified programs and activities be
implemented in full to help achieve the desirable outcomes on socio-economic
development and environmental management pursuant to the DILG-DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1; and
b. implement all projects efficiently and effectively, so that the benefits are enjoyed
by the target beneficiaries and the public on time.

We recommended that the:
• Municipal Mayor:
a. prioritize and undertake the necessary action to complete the requirements
needed in the approval of the 10 -year Solid Waste Management Plan as
prescribed by R.A 9003;
b. reorganize its Municipal Solid Waste Management Board with strict compliance
on the pre-requisite of the composition of its members, who then shall prepare,
submit and implement a plan for the safe and sanitary management of solid
waste generated in areas under in geographic coverage of Ibaan; and
c. create the position of the Municipal Environmental Officer who will take authority
over the functions, duties and responsibilities attached thereto, in coordination
with the Municipal Waste Management Board.
• Designate- Municipal Environmental Officer:
a. oversee and ensure the effective implementation of the approved MSWM Plan in
coordination with MSWMB toward compliance with the RA 9003, for the
protection and health of the constituents of the municipality and the environment
as well.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• LDRRM Officer:
a. to fasten the preparation of the Municipality’s long-term LDRRMP which
should be fully integrated with the Local Development Plan and
Comprehensive Land-Use Plan for review and evaluation of the Office of the
Civil Defense;
b. to make certain the maximum utilization of current Mitigation Funds and
coordinate with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator for the
implementation of planned programs/projects of the LDRRM Office; and
c. to ensure that the list of projects and activities which are to be charged to the
unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF- under the Trust Fund books, is
included in the Annual LDRRMF Investment Plan to be prepared yearly.
• Municipal Accountant:
a. to prepare and certified correct the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization
of LDRRMF to be submitted by the LDRRM Officer to the Audit Team on or
before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local
Development Council; and
b. to use the account Trust Liability-DRRMF account to account for the transfer of
unexpended QRF and MF-MOOE at the end of the year.
• Municipal Accountant, LDRRM Officer and Budget Officer:
a. to reconcile records regarding the outstanding of balance of the Continuing
Appropriations and total unexpended balance in the Trust Fund books;
b. to clarify and resolve what was the source of fund used on the purchase of
mini-dump truck and hydraulic cutter-spreader combination tool kit and
accordingly correct/adjust the appropriate records/accounts;
c. to make the necessary reversion of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF
amounting to ₱2,905,602.18 which remained unutilized for more than five years
from Trust Fund to General Fund after subsequent enactment of local
Sanggunian; and
d. to effect the recognition and transfer of cash from the General Fund to the
Trust Fund Book of the unutilized QRF for CYs 2018-2019 and MF-MOOE for
CY 2019, in the total amount of ₱5,486,174.63.
Considering the concurrent designation of the LDRRM Officer to the Office of the
General Services Office, we reiterated our recommendation to the Municipal Mayor
for the filling up of a permanent official on the said Office who will address the
perennial issues relative to the supplies and property management of the
Municipality to enable the LDRRM Officer to focus on the mandate of the
abovementioned regulations and other directives.

We appreciate the persistent efforts of the Municipality to support gender equality
and women empowerment, however, to address the aforementioned findings, we
recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Chairperson of the Executive
Committee together with the Gender Focal Point System and the Technical Working
Group:
a. conduct gender analysis during the preparation of GAD Plan and Budget and
Accomplishment Report using the Harmonized Gender and Development
Guidelines (HGDG) Tool;
b. secure first the Certificate of Endorsement of the Annual GAD Plan and Budget
from the DILG Provincial Office before incorporating to the Municipality’s Annual
Budget the planned PPAs to properly ascertain the relevance of the gender issues
identified and gender-responsiveness of the PPAs indicated in the GPB;
c. establish and maintain GAD Database pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC
2013-01; and
d. ensure the proper mainstreaming of GAD related activities in all
departments/offices of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate to evaluate the causes of discrepancies to ascertain the accuracy of the PPE balances in the financial statements and to maintain complete and updated PPE ledger cards; b. Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the Assets to unrecorded Fund as can be gleaned from Trust Fund as can be gleaned from Trust Fund zero for PPE; c. Inventory Committee to complete the inventory taking of all the Municipalities properties particularly on those not included in the last year and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and the Report of Unserviceable Property for submission to the Municipal Accountant and Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of every year; and d. Chairman of the Committee on Disposal, if any, to initiate/facilitate the immediate disposal of those properties in accordance with COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 in order not to expose those properties to elements that cause further deterioration or loss of salvage value.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-GSO and the LDRRMO is one and the same person and is doing his part to cope with the required work of the GSO, and the Accountant maintaining the complete and PPE ledger cards and property card.
observations.reason: Shortage or absence of personnel. The GSO Designate is at the same time the LDRRM Officer

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list from the Treasurer’s Office. b. Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for the RPT and SET Receivables and reconcile balances of the RPT, SET Receivables and their reciprocal account and total delinquent tax as prepared by the Municipal Treasurer and effect the necessary adjustments; and c. Municipal Treasurer to devise strategies to improve the collection efficiency and coordinate to produce an updated and duly verified assessment roll of the Municipality’s taxable properties and facilitate the preparation of the duly certified list showing the names of all taxpayers and the tax due from each of them at the start of the year and cause submission to the Municipal Accountant as a basis of recording in the books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables and the equivalent Deferred Income accounts.
observations.managementAction: According to the Municipal Accountant, her Office will comply with the recommendation immediately upon the receipt of assessment roll from the Municipal Assessor and Treasurer’s Office as they are awaiting for the completion of the new information system. She will then effect the necessary adjustment(s) on her records. On the other hand, the Municipal Treasurer on her letter dated August 19, 2020, specified that her Office currently relies on the commitment of the Municipal Assessor’s Office that it will provide an updated and duly verified assessment roll of the Municipality’s taxable properties. She added that her Office is committed to do the necessary reconciliation in order to come up with an accurate and reliable target. The Municipal Assessor, reiterated on her letter dated August 19, 2020, that her Office along with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office have allocated funds to establish a linked real property tax data base program that could provide reliable data on real property tax assessments and other related data concerning LGUs collection efficiency.
observations.reason: Waiting for the completion of the new information system; reliance on Municipal Assessor to provide updated assessment roll

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger cards for Local Road Networks and disclose the information for the said property in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the above cited regulations; b. General Services Office to prepare and maintain road networks property cards and to submit Report on Local Road Network; c. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and d. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s road network systems and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as of the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant stressed out that her Office will prepare the necessary ledger cards once the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects was forwarded by the Municipal Engineer to her Office. Likewise, she will prepare the required disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements accordingly. Meanwhile, the Municipal Engineer, admitted on her undated letter, that it was the first time her Office was required to submit the said report and even the effectivity and guidelines of COA Circular No. 2015-008 was new to her Office. Nevertheless, she stated that her Office will provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO of the required cost segregation at the end of the year.
observations.reason: Municipal Engineer was not aware of the requirement; awaiting provision of complete description and cost segregation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Municipal GSO-Designate to: a. number each type of supplies and maintain Stock Cards per stock number; b. forward copy of the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the DV and recording of deliveries in the appropriate ledger cards; c. consolidate weekly the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for which supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant together with the original copies of RIS; and d. submit the Report on Physical Count of Inventories to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year. • Municipal Accountant to: a. maintain Supplies Ledger Cards per stock number and record the purchases in the appropriate ledger cards upon receipt of the AIR and other supporting documents; b. use the appropriate inventory account in recording the purchases and corresponding expense account upon receipt of SSMI submitted by the GSO; and c. use the moving average method in costing the inventories.
observations.managementAction: The concerned department heads are yet to comment on the observations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accountant to: a. reconcile its records on receivables/payables in the books of the Municipality for Intra-agency accounts; b. continue the coordination with the Provincial Accounting Office until such time that the accounts be reconciled. If claims are fully documented and establishes regularity of transactions, then settlements/collections should be made by the Municipality. For those undocumented collectibles, where after all diligent efforts that had been exhausted by the Municipal Accountant still disclosed zero basis, then proper procedures leading to write-off of these accounts should be considered; and c. seek approval from the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of undocumented payables to surplus accounts after conferring with the Provincial Accountant showed the absence of any documents to prove validity of claims.
observations.managementAction: Per the Municipal Accountant, her Office will accordingly comply as recommended by the Audit Team. Personnel from her Office will be designated to handle the above accounts. The said personnel will personally follow through the nature of the reconciling items. She added that her Office will adjust her records or seek reversion from the SB of undocumented payables, if necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned municipal officials and employees to refund to the amount of ₱10,000.00 each, despite disapproval of Supplemental Budget by the Provincial Local Finance Committee as enacted by Sangguniang Panlalawigan Resolution/Ordinance No. 155 Year 2020 and contrary with the provisions of Administrative Order (AO) No. 19 dated December 2, 2019, implemented by DBM Circular No. 2019 – 6, dated December 9, 2019, on the basis that the Municipality has exceeded the 45% PS limitation.
observations.managementAction: The Management stated that the disapproval of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan came long after the said SRI has been distributed in good faith by the Municipality, as at the time it was distributed there was an approval from the Sangguniang Bayan of Ibaan. Her Office and the Treasurer, relied on the Municipal Budget Officer’s representation as to the funding source not knowing that the Municipality can no longer effect additional PS cost. As to the actual PS cost and limitation, the Management firmly believed that as of the end of the year, they have not exceeded their PS limitation after deducting waived items such as terminal leave benefits and PS of persons assigned in the economic enterprises. Moreover, the Municipal Budget Officer, on her undated letter, stated that before the year ends, her Office had prepared an Augmentation of Savings from PS of different offices to PS (SRI) of each regular employees of the Municipality totaling ₱1,174,000.00. The Municipal Mayor, then, requested from the SB an ordinance authorizing the augmentation of the said savings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer-Designate to: a. comply with the regulations that procurement of ordinary and regular office supplies and equipment involving amounts below the threshold should first be procured from DBM. Shopping from private suppliers should be resorted to, only for items not available in the PS-DBM. Thus, in case shopping is resorted to, the claim should be supported with a Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks from the PS-DBM; and b. prudently and judiciously plan procurement for each quarter as consolidated and incorporate them in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) in order to adopt competitive public bidding to provide reasonable assurance that the best quality at the lowest price is availed.
observations.managementAction: The GSO-Designate, on his letter dated August 20, 2020, reasoned out the lack of storage area for the supplies being requested by different Offices when they moved out of their permanent Office during the rehabilitation of the Municipal Building early 2019. Hence, they come up with shopping as the mode of procurement, instead of the required procurement from the PS-DBM, just to cater the needs of different Offices and to fit in on the available small storage space. He added that purchasing from PS-DBM entails high cost of truck rental and most of the time, quality of supplies required by the Municipality is not available. Nonetheless, he stressed out that his Office will comply with Audit Team’s recommendation to procure ordinary and regular office supplies from DBM first and will eventually secure Certificate of Non-Availability of stocks for items not offered therein.
observations.reason: Lack of storage area during the rehabilitation of the Municipal Building; cost of truck rental and perceived non-availability of items at PS-DBM

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. require the Municipal Accountant to prepare individual financial statements with complete subsidiary ledgers for each economic enterprise being operated by the Municipality and for its 20% Development Fund which should form part of the schedules of the General Fund in accordance with the above cited regulations; and b. instruct the Municipal Budget Officer in coordination with the Head of the economic enterprise to continuously appropriate different funds for Waterworks and Cemetery but separate the appropriation for Market and Slaughterhouse in the ensuing year to facilitate the reconciliation with the records of the Accounting office.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant stated that her Office is preparing separate records for the income and expense of the above economic enterprises for the subsequent preparation of its income statement. However, in view of the foregoing, to fully comply with recommendation, she believed that there is also a need for the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to maintain a separate cash books and separate appropriations and obligations, respectively. She added that since the Municipality is planning for a fully-computerized operations, they will request the programmer to customize such and hopefully, next year, will come up with the complete books of accounts for the above economic enterprises and 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: Need for Municipal Treasurer and Budget Officer to maintain separate cash books and appropriations; awaiting computerization/customization

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Development Coordinator to: a. ensure the 20% Local Development Fund appropriated out of Internal Revenue Allotment be optimally utilized and identified programs and activities be implemented in full to help achieve the desirable outcomes on socio-economic development and environmental management pursuant to the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1; and b. implement all projects efficiently and effectively, so that the benefits are enjoyed by the target beneficiaries and the public on time.
observations.managementAction: The Management is yet to reply on the observations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: • Municipal Mayor: a. prioritize and undertake the necessary action to complete the requirements needed in the approval of the 10 -year Solid Waste Management Plan as prescribed by R.A 9003; b. reorganize its Municipal Solid Waste Management Board with strict compliance on the pre-requisite of the composition of its members, who then shall prepare, submit and implement a plan for the safe and sanitary management of solid waste generated in areas under in geographic coverage of Ibaan; and c. create the position of the Municipal Environmental Officer who will take authority over the functions, duties and responsibilities attached thereto, in coordination with the Municipal Waste Management Board. • Designate- Municipal Environmental Officer: a. oversee and ensure the effective implementation of the approved MSWM Plan in coordination with MSWMB toward compliance with the RA 9003, for the protection and health of the constituents of the municipality and the environment as well.
observations.managementAction: The Management informed the Audit Team that they will accordingly comply with the recommendation. As such, they will prioritize the approval of their 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan. They will also reorganize their Solid Waste Management Board and will create a position for the Municipal Environmental Officer as soon as their PS limitation is no longer exceeded, hopefully by the year 2021.
observations.reason: Awaiting ability to create position due to PS limitation; plan to prioritize approval and reorganization

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • LDRRM Officer: a. to fasten the preparation of the Municipality’s long-term LDRRMP which should be fully integrated with the Local Development Plan and Comprehensive Land-Use Plan for review and evaluation of the Office of the Civil Defense; b. to make certain the maximum utilization of current Mitigation Funds and coordinate with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator for the implementation of planned programs/projects of the LDRRM Office; and c. to ensure that the list of projects and activities which are to be charged to the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF- under the Trust Fund books, is included in the Annual LDRRMF Investment Plan to be prepared yearly. • Municipal Accountant: a. to prepare and certified correct the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to be submitted by the LDRRM Officer to the Audit Team on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council; and b. to use the account Trust Liability-DRRMF account to account for the transfer of unexpended QRF and MF-MOOE at the end of the year. • Municipal Accountant, LDRRM Officer and Budget Officer: a. to reconcile records regarding the outstanding of balance of the Continuing Appropriations and total unexpended balance in the Trust Fund books;
