b. to clarify and resolve what was the source of fund used on the purchase of mini-dump truck and hydraulic cutter-spreader combination tool kit and accordingly correct/adjust the appropriate records/accounts; c. to make the necessary reversion of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF amounting to ₱2,905,602.18 which remained unutilized for more than five years from Trust Fund to General Fund after subsequent enactment of local Sanggunian; and d. to effect the recognition and transfer of cash from the General Fund to the Trust Fund Book of the unutilized QRF for CYs 2018-2019 and MF-MOOE for CY 2019, in the total amount of ₱5,486,174.63.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant stated her adherence to the recommendation regarding the preparation of the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to be submitted on or before the 15th day after the end of each month. Moreover, she will use the account Trust Liability-DRRMF to account for the transfer of unexpended QRF and MF-MOOE. She further quoted that reconciliation of records regarding the proper amount of Continuing Appropriations and total unexpended balance in the Trust Fund books will be performed in order to determine the real source of funds on the purchase of mini dump truck and hydraulic cutter-spreader combination tool kit. Necessary reversion of unexpended amount aged more than five years from the Trust Fund books to the General Fund books will be made as soon as possible, the same with the transfer for the unutilized QRF in CYs 2018-2019 and MF-MOOE in CY 2019.
observations.reason: Concurrent designation of LDRRM Officer to the Office of the General Services; need to fill permanent official to address perennial issues

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Chairperson of the Executive Committee together with the Gender Focal Point System and the Technical Working Group: a. conduct gender analysis during the preparation of GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report using the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) Tool; b. secure first the Certificate of Endorsement of the Annual GAD Plan and Budget from the DILG Provincial Office before incorporating to the Municipality’s Annual Budget the planned PPAs to properly ascertain the relevance of the gender issues identified and gender-responsiveness of the PPAs indicated in the GPB; c. establish and maintain GAD Database pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2013-01; and d. ensure the proper mainstreaming of GAD related activities in all departments/offices of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: According to the Gender Focal Person, on her letter dated August 20, 2020, GAD Database was established and maintained pursuant to the above JMC. She added that the GFPS will promptly answer the comments of the DILG on GPB on the next time so that they could acquire a Certificate of Endorsement from them. Likewise, she promised to conduct gender analysis during the preparation of GPB and AR using the HGDG tool. Lastly, they will ensure the proper mainstreaming of GAD related activities in all department of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor provide the Municipal Treasurer with the assessment roll of the Municipal real property and the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with the certified list of real property taxpayers indicating the amount due and collectibles to serve as basis in setting up the receivable at the beginning of the year. Also, reconcile the difference between the Schedule of Tax Delinquencies with the balances of RPT/SET Receivables in the financial statements and draw adjusting entries to come up with the correct balance.
observations.managementAction: No action was done yet; Municipal Treasurer has just assumed Office and the OIC-Treasurer was not able to furnish the Accountant the certified list earlier.
observations.reason: No action was done due to turnover and personnel changes; the current Treasurer only recently assumed duties and is in process of reconciling.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Municipal Accountant and GSO Designate to evaluate the causes of
discrepancies to ascertain the accuracy of the PPE balances in the
financial statements and maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards
and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of both
records;
b) Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the unrecorded assets
arising from Trust Fund for the current and prior periods;
c) Inventory Committee to conduct complete inventory taking of all the
Municipal properties particularly on those not included in the last year and
prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) and the Report of Unserviceable Property for submission to the
Municipal Accountant and Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of
every year; and
d) Chairman of the Committee on Disposal, if any, to initiate/facilitate the
immediate disposal of those properties in accordance with COA Circular
No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 in order not to expose those properties
to elements that cause further deterioration or loss of salvage value.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Accountant to
a. Reconcile the difference between the balances of Real Property Tax
(RPT) Receivable and Special Education Tax (SET) Receivable;
b. Maintain subsidiary ledgers for the RPT and SET Receivables in order
to validate the balances of the accounts presented in the financial
statements and to ensure accuracy and reliability of the balances
reported monthly;
Acting Municipal Treasurer to
a. submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list showing the name
of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectibles at the beginning
of the year as basis for the setting up of receivables to ensure reliability
of the balances of Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education
Tax Receivable accounts;
b. devise strategies to improve the collection efficiency of the Municipality
based on the reliable data set-up of the real property tax receivables; and
c. monitor payment of real property tax due for the current year and
exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all delinquent real
property taxes and encourage the tax payer to pay their dues on time.
Conduct verification on those outstanding balances to determine the
existence of the receivables and its collectibility.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
her staff to continuously coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and reconcile
their records on their reciprocal receivables/payables in the books of the Province
to establish the correct balances in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers and to
ascertain the legality and validity of both claims so both parties may make the
necessary adjustments on their respective books of accounts, We further
recommend that immediate reconciliation be done every time a transaction
occurs to avoid the recurrence of unreconciled discrepancies.

Likewise, effect the immediate settlement of obligations of each fund for a fair
presentation of the cash position for the three funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Municipal
Officials to undertake careful planning and execution of the proposed annual
budget on the basis of realistic projection of revenues for the ensuing year and
conduct proper review of past collection performances.

Likewise, we recommended that periodic assessment should be made by the
concerned officials in the Municipality to ascertain whether collection targets are
attained. The reasons for the shortfall in collections should be analyzed and
alternative actions should be looked into to avoid incurrence of misses in
projections in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the employees to refund the
total amount of P600,000.00.

For full adoption of PRAISE providing concrete basis for future grants of
incentives, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor and Praise Committee:
a) comply strictly with the policy, rules and regulations on the Civil Service
Commission’s Program on Awards and Incentives for Service Excellence
pursuant to CSC Memorandum Circular No. 01 s 2001 to ensure propriety and
regularity of any actions taken out of the said Program.
b) ensure that the rules, specific policies and guidelines in performance planning,
monitoring and evaluation and mechanism for recognizing the awardees are in
place before granting awards to the officials and employees.
c) consider individual appraisal or evaluation report assessing the performance
the employees by means of the agency’s established system of incentives and
awards.
Ensure that monetary awards were given only when the accomplishments and
other personal efforts resulted in monetary savings and it does not exceed 20% of
the savings generated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a) in coordination with the HRMO, regularly reconcile employees’ records at GSIS,
PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG to identify the affected employees on the
unremitted balances and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies;
b) identify the cause/s of the over remittance for some accounts and make the proper
adjustments; and
c) remit the due GSIS loan amortizations pursuant to Section 14.2, Rule III of the
Implementing Rules and Regulations of Government Service Insurance Act of
1997 or R.A. No. 8291 to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary financial
expenses in the form of imposed penalties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to prepare an
updated master list indicating the economic status of each senior citizen. The
MSWDO must ensure that all the eligible and qualified senior citizens are duly
selected taking into account the order of priority and economic qualification
provided under the DSWD Administrative Order No. 15 series of 2010 dated
December 13, 2010. Finally, conduct quarterly validation and monitoring to the
beneficiaries to ascertain their entitlement and the propriety of the utilization of
the stipend for the full implementation of Social Pension Program of the
government.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
a) Municipal Budget Officer appropriate in its annual budget no less than twenty
percent (20%) of its annual internal revenue allotment for development
projects;
b) Local Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development
Office (MPDO), together with the concerned offices, immediately look into the
unimplemented projects under the 20% Development Fund and determine
causes of their non-implementation. Formulate/develop specific strategies/
solutions to effectively implement development projects. Consider formulating
a timetable within which to complete the unimplemented projects;
c) The Municipal Engineering Office improve the planning and scheduling of
work activities and manpower in order to facilitate completion of the Program
of Work of unimplemented projects and vigorously pursue the implementation
the soonest time possible in order to achieve the objectives of the said
projects in providing socio-economic benefits to the intended beneficiaries;
and
d) The MPDC enhance monitoring of implementation of projects and to submit
regularly status reports of implementation to the Municipal Mayor for his
information so that appropriate measures can be instituted to obtain optimal
utilization of fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to prepare and submit to the Local
Sanggunian through the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council
and Local Development Council the annual LDRRMO Plan and budget specifying
in detail the list of programs, activities and projects which include under a
separate caption those projects and programs charged to the unexpended
LDRRMF of previous years, and implement at the intended period of its
implementation to ensure that the purpose for which the fund was established are
met.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant expedite the activation of
Trust Fund account maintained with the Land Bank-Rosario branch for the
immediate transfer of corresponding net cash equivalent of unexpended balance
of ₱2,400,000.00 QRF for CY 2018, and the amount of P2,774,544.63 utilized for
supplemental budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Office of the Human
Resource Management and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to discontinue the
practice of allowing deductions on monthly loan amortizations of government/
private lending institutions and capital contribution to employees’ association
from the personnel’s payroll other than those mandated by law. Strictly comply
with the GAA provision that monthly take-home pay of employee shall not be
lower than Five Thousand Pesos (₱5,000.00).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the chairperson of the
Municipal Council for the Protection of Children (MCPC):
a) instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate 1% share from IRA for LCPC
as provided in DILG MC No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 for the implementation
LCPC;
b) direct the members of the Council to prepare the Local Development Plan for
Children (LDPC) and Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC), and maximize
utilization of the allocated LCPC Fund to identified programs, projects and
activities (PPAs) provided for in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120
dated July 4, 2012 to ensure full strengthening and implementation of the
LCPC; and
c) require the Municipal Social Welfare Officer, being the focal person, to ensure
that the identified PPAs geared for promotion of rights and welfare of children
are carried out effectively and efficiently and continuously monitor the
implementation of children’s programs and projects in the locality.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Office of the MENRO will expedite
the processing of the requirements of the returned 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan,
for an effective implementation of the solid waste management program.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the GAD Focal Person will secure first
the Certificate of Endorsement of the Annual Gender and Development (GAD) Plan and
Budget (GPB) from the DILG Provincial Office before incorporating to the Municipality’s
Annual Budget to properly ascertain the relevance of the gender issues identified and
gender-responsiveness of the PPAs indicated in the GPB. Further, Management committed
to establish the GAD Database to help strengthen the planning and budgeting processes for
GAD.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Instruct the General Services Officer-Designate (GSO) to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE of the Municipality to identify and prove the existence of the assets. b. require the Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate to maintain ledgers and property cards and reconcile their respective records on property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) and effect adjustments accordingly. c. Inclusion of unserviceable properties with undetermined value in the list of PPE and dispose the noted unserviceable property in accordance with PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 92-386 dated Oct. 20, 1992 to recover certain amount as other income for LGU and to avoid further deterioration and possible loss of the property.
observations.managementAction: The GSO conducted a complete physical inventory of ARE’s on file to determine existence of such PPEs and submit the RPCPPE.
observations.reason: Non-proper turn-over of personnel due sudden illness/death.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference between the balances of Real Property Tax (RPT) Receivable and Special Education Tax (SET) Receivable; maintain subsidiary ledgers for the RPT and SET Receivables in order to validate the balances of the accounts presented in the financial statements and to ensure accuracy and reliability of the balances reported monthly; and the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectibles at the beginning of the year as basis for the setting up of receivables to ensure reliability of the balances of Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts; devise strategies to improve the collection efficiency of the Municipality based on the reliable data set-up of the real property tax receivables; and monitor payment of real property tax due for the current year and exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all delinquent real property taxes and encourage the tax payer to pay their dues on time. Conduct verification on those outstanding balances to determine the existence of the receivables and its collectibility.
observations.managementAction: Instructions were already given to the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to correct receivables set up in prior years so that the recorded amounts of receivables may arrive at a more realistic amount; and to maintain subsidiary ledgers for current and prior years for facility of verification.
observations.reason: The IT section is in the process of generating the list of taxpayers. Lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to: design effective tax strategies to intensify collection of Real Property Taxes, not only current taxes but also the delinquent taxes in order to generate more income; and enforce the tax remedies provided for under Book II of the Local Government Code in order to improve the collection efficiency.
observations.managementAction: Sent demand letters to delinquent taxpayers as preliminaries for filing of cases by the provincial government; additional collection measures were taken.
observations.reason: There were payments made on the accounts and some remedies provided under Book II of the Local Government Code cannot be enforced locally by the municipality as collection actions are also done by the provincial government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately effect adjustments of these long outstanding reconciling items in the books of accounts for a fair presentation of cash balances; b) Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the necessary adjustment in the books of the Municipality in order to reflect the correct and reliable balances of the Cash in Bank-Local Currency-Time Deposit accounts and to prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts on a monthly basis; c) ensure that supporting documents requiring recording and adjustments in the books are secured and recorded immediately in the ensuing month and banks are immediately informed of any bank errors for their immediate action.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer were already directed to adjust long outstanding reconciling items in the books of accounts; Journal Entry Voucher and necessary adjusting entry were prepared for Accounts Payable as of December 31, 2017 accordingly.
observations.reason: Supporting documents were secured and adjustments were recorded as per instruction of the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to remit the unexpended funds to the source agencies or request approval from the latter to use the money for other vital projects of the municipality and comply with procedural requirements.
observations.managementAction: Majority of those projects were already implemented in the year they were received. Some funds arrived near the last quarter and were used for other purposes since the fund is not enough to fully develop the project. Funds reverted in some cases as per guidelines.
observations.reason: Not all were implemented; some procedural requirements (e.g., RA 9184) needed to be complied with and timing of fund receipt affected implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to perform periodic reconciliation of Due to LGUs and Due from LGUs and reconcile the receivable from the corresponding payable accounts and vice-versa to reflect the correct balances of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation process is now being done on a regular basis. The Municipal Accountant is regularly reconciling with the Provincial books.
observations.reason: Timing differences always exist and personnel constraints caused delays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the payable accounts; determine the validity of the accounts and request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of those long outstanding payables to unappropriated surplus, and prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reflect the correct Accounts Payable as of December 31, 2017.
observations.managementAction: The accounts payable were reviewed and analyzed and adjusting entry was prepared to reflect the correct balances.
observations.reason: There were still payments on some of the amounts and possibility of outstanding claims related to those accounts required retention; therefore not all were reverted immediately.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to verify and evaluate the accounts involved in the above transactions and to prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect the adjustments for the stale checks to present the correct balance of the Cash in Bank and Accounts Payable accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The necessary adjustments were effected and the stale checks were evaluated; entries were prepared.
observations.reason: The stale checks were addressed and adjustments were made; no further action required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The amounts were adjusted, paid and recorded in the books.
observations.reason: The necessary adjustments were made and the obligations were settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Local Development Council ensure proper planning and realignment as necessary, review and develop a feasible Annual Investment Plan towards the socio-economic development and ensure accomplishment of the programs and projects on-going and yet to be implemented.
observations.managementAction: Assigned personnel reviewed and some projects were realigned; some were implemented while others were pending conversion or budget issues.
observations.reason: Some projects were not yet implemented and were pending transfer/donation of properties or further documentation; timing and budget constraints affected implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor fully utilize the BUB funds for the benefit of the constituents and ensure the proper use thereof in accordance with the guidelines under DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 7.
observations.managementAction: It was requested to the DILG to be used for other purposes as the fund is not enough to fully develop the spring. The Joint project was implemented and paid subsequently.
observations.reason: The project took some time before implementation and the request for re-purposing was approved; funds were utilized accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC) to properly plan and fully implement the DRRM programs/activities and to ensure that the funds were utilized in accordance with approved budget and plans.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMC and LDRRMO were required to properly plan and implement the projects and programs. Measures to ensure utilization were undertaken.
observations.reason: Minimal implementation due to delayed procurement and project location negotiations with provincial government; some projects not yet implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to submit GAD Plans and Budget to the Provincial Planning and DILG Regional Office for review and approval and to establish a GAD database as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction: GAD Focal Person to spearhead the preparation and submission of required documents. Some submissions were made; Municipality to establish database.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel and pending DILG review/endorsement; some approvals not yet released.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials adopt measures and strategies to improve collection efficiency and enforce the remedies provided under Sections 254, 256 and 259 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent and measures were taken to enforce remedies; however, enforcement through some remedies requires provincial action.
observations.reason: Enforcement at the municipal level limited; some measures require provincial action; collection efforts continuing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze and reconcile non-moving balances and that the Sangguniang Bayan pass a Resolution requesting proper write-off of the receivables from KKK proponents and submit proof of efforts exerted to recover the said amount.
observations.managementAction: Review and reconciliation were done; amounts have been reduced as some records were clarified.
observations.reason: Old records and lack of documentation slowed the write-off process; further reconciliation ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the previous entries for all funds under the Due from/to Other Funds accounts to reconcile and reflect the correct balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is reconciling the accounts; reconciliation is being done with supporting documents.
observations.reason: Timing differences and unavailable records caused continuing discrepancies; reconciliation in process.

observations.recommendation: a. Instruct the Inventory Committee to coordinate with the General Services Officer-Designate to conduct a complete physical count and submit the RPCPPE in the prescribed format to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year. b. Require the Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate to reconcile their records and maintain ledgers and property cards. c. Dispose unserviceable properties in accordance with applicable rules and prepare necessary journal entries to derecognize disposed assets.
observations.managementAction: Inventory and RPCPPE preparation were undertaken; some reconciliations made; disposal procedures to be followed.
observations.reason: RPCPPE not submitted in complete prescribed format previously; lack of personnel delayed full reconciliation and disposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to pursue the titling of the 14 parcels in the name of the Municipality and the Municipal Budget to provide appropriation for the cost of titling the land.
observations.managementAction: Assigned staff to process the required documents and a liaison officer was designated to process the documents; reconsidered for budget allocation.
observations.reason: Processing of documents in initial stage; allocation for titling to be provided in budget; some titles still in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require proper planning and implementation of LDRRMF projects, submission of annual LDRRMO plan and budget, and preparation of detailed list of programs/activities including those charged to unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.
observations.managementAction: LDRRMO to prepare and submit annual plans to LDRRMC and LDC; meetings to be conducted; the activation of trust fund account initiated.
observations.reason: Delayed procurement and negotiation for project sites; some projects pending provincial coordination; trust fund account activation pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor include among top priorities the construction of a building for the safekeeping and accommodation of documents to be submitted to the audit team, with consideration of the ten (10) year preservation period.
observations.managementAction: A building or storage space was provided and the Municipal Hall made available for safekeeping; the Municipality provided space for the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Space for safekeeping was provided and available for the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) to review and revise the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan and submit to the Provincial government for approval and to ensure establishment of functional MRFs in barangays.
observations.managementAction: The 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan was revised and resubmitted and the MRFs in barangays were established; the MSWMB was reorganized to conform with requirements.
observations.reason: The plan was revised and implementation of MRFs accomplished; some approvals were pending but addressed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the disclosure requirements in preparing the Notes to Financial Statements to assist users in understanding the financial statements and in decision-making.
observations.managementAction: Accordingly complied as recommended by the audit team.
observations.reason: Required disclosures were updated and presented per PPSASs and COA circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide and maintain a complete set of subsidiary ledger for each special account and to submit trial balances and financial statements pursuant to NGAS Sections 105 and 110.
observations.managementAction: Corresponding subsidiary ledgers were maintained and reports submitted as required.
observations.reason: Complied as required by the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the GSO to submit RPCPPE in prescribed format; the Municipal Accountant and GSO to reconcile records and dispose unserviceable properties; prepare necessary journal entries.
observations.managementAction: RPCPPE and reconciliations were undertaken though some RPCPPE were not submitted in complete format initially; adjustments and disposals to follow procedures.
observations.reason: RPCPPE was not fully submitted in the prescribed format; work in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that programs and projects under the Mitigation Fund be implemented immediately and that LDRRM plans be formulated and funded.
observations.managementAction: Plans were to be prepared and submitted; some projects delayed due to location negotiation with the Provincial Government.
observations.reason: Delayed implementation due to project site negotiations and funding timing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish certified list and that the Municipal Accountant reconcile differences and adjust receivables accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer who assumed office later was in process of reconciling with the Assessor and providing certified lists.
observations.reason: Change in personnel and turnover delayed reconciliation; current Treasurer working on it.

observations.recommendation: We recommended submission of GPB to DILG for endorsement prior to integration in annual budget and establishment of GAD database.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person to secure endorsement and establish database; some measures were initiated.
observations.reason: Pending approvals and endorsement from DILG; some elements still to be established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended enforcement of remedies under RA 7160 and adoption of measures to improve collection efficiency.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters and other measures were undertaken; enforcement is ongoing.
observations.reason: Some remedies require provincial assistance; efforts ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended reconciliation, analysis, and submission of resolution for write-off where applicable with proof of collection efforts.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation performed and amounts reduced; further actions pending Sangguniang Bayan resolution for write-off.
observations.reason: Old records and documentation gaps delayed completion of write-off procedures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended reconciliation of Due from/to Other Funds to reflect correct balances.
observations.managementAction: Regular reconciliation with supporting documents is being done.
observations.reason: Differences remain due to timing and missing documentation; reconciliation ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) require the General
Services Officer-Designate (GSO) to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE
of the Municipality to identify and validate the existence of the recorded PPEs
and submit the corresponding RPCPPE in the prescribed format to the auditor; and
b) require the Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate to maintain ledgers
and property cards and reconcile their respective records and effect adjustments
accordingly.

We again recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
- Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the
certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due
and collectibles for the year as provided in Section 20, Volume I of the
NGAS Manual in order to ensure reliability of the balances of Real

We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile the difference between the Schedule of Tax Delinquencies with the balances of RPT/SET Receivables in the financial statements and draw adjusting entries to come up with the correct balance of the receivable and deferred income accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Accounting had reconciled their records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all year-end collections on the next banking day. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer adopt an internal control system on the monitoring of collections and faithfully comply with the above rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: All year-end and holidays collections were deposited the first Working/banking day of January 2016. The Municipal Treasurer's Office is continuously monitoring collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts of Due from LGU and Due to LGU with the books of the Province so that the necessary adjustments could be made to correct balances of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: Accounting is still in the process of reconciling these reciprocal accounts.
observations.reason: No records for old accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the assistance of the Social Welfare and Development Officer and the Local Finance Committee cause the formulation of a clear-cut policies and guidelines on its program of granting donations and financial assistance for ratification of the SB. The beneficiaries, amounts and documentary requirements and the processing office/s should be well defined and aligned with DSWD guidelines to ensure that qualified beneficiaries are assisted and that assistance is given only for public and general welfare purpose's.
observations.managementAction: Management is adopting the guidclines on financial assistance issue by the DSWD-National Office No. 04 series of 2015 dated March 6, 2015.
observations.reason: Not all amounts recorded in the Donations - accounts

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Person and other concerned municipal officials utilize the fund optimally and solely for the programs/projects/activities that intend to address the gender gaps or issues faced by men and women of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted programs/activities which they considered GAD projects/activities which they considered GAD related and which all have gender issues as they are reflected in the CBMS.
observations.reason: As far as they were concerned, all projects/activities were all GAD related.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA 9003: complete the 10 year Solid Waste Management Plan; pass a resolution with the barangay to establish material recovery facilities; establish the MRF for composting or recycling; encourage the barangay to monitor MRF before transferring the residual wastes that would receive mixed waste for final sorting; and other prescribed actions including requiring refund of excess payment of garbage tipping fee.
observations.managementAction: The 10 year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan is already submitted. The Municipality has established a central MRF located at Barangay Balanga, Ibaan, Batangas. All 26 barangays are currently establishing their own MRF. The GSO-designate is waiting for the final computation from Taysan Solid Wastes Management, Incorporated before refund of the excess payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist and other concerned Municipal Officials exert more effort to monitor the status of breeding animals and to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the details of this account. Likewise, require the accountable officer to file Request for relief from accountability for those dead animals.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Agriculture Office monitored the status of breeding animals and submitted the same to the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer adopt measures and strategies on how to improve collection efficiency. Coordinated efforts in the implementation of real property tax program should be applied in the Municipality and barangay levels to maximize collection of taxes. If in case all the measures were undertaken but still the collection efficiency is below par, avail of the remedies and sanctions as provided in Section 256 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer's Office prepared and delivered the letters/notices/statements of tax liabilities to real property taxpayers.
observations.reason: Tax delinquencies increased despite effort exerted
